name: Pre-Sold Author Package: Frequently Asked Questions
description: Every question a prospective author asks about the six-month Pre-Sold Author Package, the Legacy Publishing JV, the knowledge panel build, the pre-sell audience mechanics, and the application process.

mainEntity.name: What does the $30,000 cover?

acceptedAnswer.text: The $30,000 covers all four pillars delivered on a single 180-day clock. Book production through Legacy Publishing (ghostwriting or manuscript-in-hand, five-pass edit, cover, interior layout, ARC copies, blurbs, retail distribution). Podcast launch and infrastructure inside the PodcastNetwork.org network (branding, hosting, distribution to Apple Podcasts and Spotify, launch cadence). Knowledge panel work (Wikidata Q-number seeding, Entity Home construction, structured data markup, Google Knowledge Graph indexing). The pre-sell sequence (12 to 15 top-tier podcast guest appearances, waitlist mechanics, target 5,000+ pre-orders locked before launch day). One price, one contract, six months. Legacy Publishing takes 10% of net book sales as royalty on the resulting title. No upfront fee to Legacy beyond the $30,000 paid to PodcastNetwork.org.

mainEntity.name: How can you deliver in six months when the industry standard is 12 to 24 months?

acceptedAnswer.text: The industry standard is 12 to 24 months because the book, the podcast, the knowledge panel, and the audience are treated as four separate purchases from four separate vendors, run in sequence. The Pre-Sold Author Package runs them in parallel from Day 1 under one operator. Podcast infrastructure feeds the knowledge panel signals. Podcast guest appearances feed the audience build. The manuscript work runs alongside all of it, not after. Because the four pillars produce compounding signal for each other, the calendar compresses to 180 days. This is not a shortcut. It is a different architecture that only works because the four pillars are designed to feed each other from the first week.

mainEntity.name: What if I already have a manuscript?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bring it. Same $30,000 price, same 180-day timeline, same four pillars. The manuscript-in-hand path runs the five-pass edit (developmental, structural, line, copy, proof) with Legacy Publishing's editorial team, then straight into cover design, interior layout, and production. Edited manuscript is typically complete Day 60 to 75, which frees calendar time that reallocates to the podcast, the knowledge panel, and the pre-sell sequence. Ghostwriting path authors have a Day 90 handoff; manuscript-in-hand authors have a slightly earlier one. The launch date is the same.

mainEntity.name: Do you take every author who applies?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Application-only. PodcastNetwork.org accepts a limited number of authors each quarter because six-month builds require a full team on each project. Fit criteria: an existing 5,000+ mailing list, 25,000+ monthly podcast downloads, an activated retail channel through your role or professional network, or a clear platform-in-progress with real traction. Authors starting from zero are not a fit for this package. Applications are reviewed within two business days.

mainEntity.name: What if I don't have a podcast yet?

acceptedAnswer.text: The package includes launching your show inside the PodcastNetwork.org network in the first 30 days. Show branding, cover art, hosting setup, distribution across Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and the major directories, and the sequenced launch cadence are all inside the $30,000 price. If you already have a podcast, PodcastNetwork.org works with the existing show rather than launching a new one, and the freed calendar hours reallocate to the pre-sell sequence and the knowledge panel work.

mainEntity.name: How does the Legacy Publishing JV work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Legacy Publishing is the elite application-only publishing partner on the Pre-Sold Author Package. Manuscript handoff happens at Day 90. From there, Legacy runs cover design, interior layout, ARC copies, blurb sourcing, and retail distribution. Legacy takes 10% of net book sales as royalty on the resulting title. There is no upfront fee to Legacy beyond the $30,000 paid to PodcastNetwork.org. Legacy is application-only in its own right. Admission to the Pre-Sold Author Package includes admission to Legacy for the resulting book. Full disclosure of the JV mechanics lives on the Legacy JV Disclosure page.

mainEntity.name: How does the Google Knowledge Panel get built inside 180 days?

acceptedAnswer.text: The knowledge panel work runs in parallel from Day 1. Wikidata Q-number seeding starts in week one, which is the primary structured signal Google's Knowledge Graph consumes. The Entity Home (the canonical, author-owned URL that Google treats as the ground-truth reference for the author entity) is built Day 14 to 30 with the full JSON-LD Person and Book schema stack live at launch. Schema.org markup ships across the Entity Home, the publisher page at Legacy, the podcast, and the guest-appearance destinations. Google Knowledge Graph typically ingests a well-seeded Wikidata entity within 60 to 120 days. The knowledge panel appears once the Graph accepts the entity and Google surfaces it in SERP. On this build the panel typically lands between Day 90 and Day 180.

mainEntity.name: How do you get to 5,000+ pre-orders before launch day?

acceptedAnswer.text: The pre-sell sequence is 12 to 15 top-tier podcast guest appearances in the author's category, booked and sequenced across Days 30 to 150. Every appearance drives listeners to a waitlist landing page with a book-preview lead magnet. The waitlist mechanic converts list signups into pre-orders in the final 60 days through a warmup and pre-order cadence. Pre-order counts scale in the final third because that is when the podcast catalog, the knowledge panel authority, and the guest-appearance funnel are all producing at peak. Target 5,000+ pre-orders locked before launch day. Actual numbers vary by category and platform strength coming in. Authors with stronger existing platforms typically exceed target. Authors on the platform floor typically land near target.

mainEntity.name: Do you take international authors?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Legacy Publishing distributes in North America, the United Kingdom, and select international markets. The knowledge panel work is global (Wikidata and Google Knowledge Graph are language-agnostic and market-agnostic). The podcast guest-booking sequence is strongest in North America and the United Kingdom. Authors targeting other primary markets should flag that in the application so PodcastNetwork.org can validate fit before intake.

mainEntity.name: Can I add speaking engagements to the package?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not inside the six-month build. Speaking bureau work sits outside the 180-day sequence. A published author with a live knowledge panel and a 5,000+ pre-sold list tends to attract inbound speaking interest on its own, and PodcastNetwork.org can point you to bureau partners after launch. Speaking is a downstream revenue stream that the package sets up, not a deliverable inside the package.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I miss the Day 90 manuscript handoff?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Day 90 handoff is the critical path. If your side of the manuscript slips (ghostwriting interviews delayed, edits returned late, source material incomplete), the launch date slips proportionally. PodcastNetwork.org flags risk at Day 60 in a formal check-in. Most slips are recoverable inside the window. Structural slips get flagged early enough to reset the launch date to a still-defensible target. The pre-sell sequence and the podcast keep compounding while the manuscript catches up, which means a slipped launch date typically arrives at a stronger audience.

mainEntity.name: How does payment work?

acceptedAnswer.text: $30,000 one-time, paid up front to PodcastNetwork.org after application acceptance and contract signing. Payment processes through GHL Payments on Stripe Connect. Contract signing happens through GHL Documents. There is no monthly retainer, no hidden fee, and no additional cost due to Legacy Publishing beyond the standing 10% of net book sales royalty on the resulting title.

mainEntity.name: Is there a refund policy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because the six-month build begins with a full team commitment on Day 1, the $30,000 is non-refundable after the kickoff call. Between contract signing and the kickoff call (typically within one week of contract), a full refund is available. In the case of catastrophic non-delivery by PodcastNetwork.org after the kickoff call, the remedy sits in the contract. Detailed terms are in the master services agreement provided at intake.

mainEntity.name: Who is this package NOT for?

acceptedAnswer.text: Authors starting from zero platform. Authors who want a traditional publishing engagement without the pre-sell sequence attached (those authors are pointed at Legacy Publishing's application-only intake directly). Authors who want editorial-only support at a lower price point (those authors are pointed at Ally Publishing's editing tiers). Fiction authors, memoir authors without a professional platform anchor, and authors who want to keep the book work separate from the audience work all fall outside the package's design.

mainEntity.name: What is AI or Die and how does it relate to the package?

acceptedAnswer.text: AI or Die is the co-authored book by Brett K Moore and Mike Partners, the co-founders of PodcastNetwork.org, published under the Legacy Publishing JV. It is the case study PodcastNetwork.org ran on itself before selling the Pre-Sold Author Package externally. The book is the founder-eating-own-cooking proof that the six-month arc produces the promised four pillars. AI or Die's launch is the anchor case study on the website, and every deliverable inside the package (book, podcast, knowledge panel, pre-sold audience) is documented on the AI or Die case study page.

mainEntity.name: What happens after Day 180?

acceptedAnswer.text: The package concludes at Day 180 with the book launch and a 30-day post-launch report. From there, the author owns the book (published under the Legacy JV structure), the podcast (which continues to publish independently), the Entity Home and knowledge panel, and the pre-sold audience list. Optional continued podcast production, ongoing audience growth, and speaking-bureau introduction are available under separate agreement post-launch. The default state at Day 180 is a fully-launched, fully-owned authority platform that keeps compounding without PodcastNetwork.org running it.

mainEntity.name: How long is the application process?

acceptedAnswer.text: Applications take about 10 minutes to complete. PodcastNetwork.org responds within two business days. Accepted applicants get a discovery call within one week. Contract and payment finalize within two weeks. Kickoff typically lands three to four weeks after application submission for accepted authors. Day 1 of the 180-day clock is the kickoff call, not the application date.

mainEntity.name: What would it cost to run the same play on my own?

acceptedAnswer.text: The DIY path lands somewhere north of $50,000 in vendor costs at the low end and takes two to three years. A freelance five-pass edit runs $8,000 to $15,000. Cover and interior design runs $3,000 to $7,000. A standalone knowledge panel engagement with the industry standard vendor runs 12 to 24 months at $30,000 to $80,000. A launch PR retainer runs $8,000 to $15,000 per month for three to six months. Guest-booking services run $3,000 to $7,000 per month with mixed results. And the four workstreams are not coordinated with each other, which means the launch lands with four disconnected deliverables that were never designed to compound. The Pre-Sold Author Package produces the same four deliverables in six months, coordinated by design, at $30,000 flat.

mainEntity.name: Who is currently in-launch and where can I see results?

acceptedAnswer.text: Three authors are currently in the in-launch cohort: Michele Okimura, Dominic Jones, and Rob Okimura. Each has a case study page on the site with the specific six-month plan for that author, the metrics being tracked (launch-list target, pre-order goal, launch-week target, knowledge-panel target), and a quarterly update log that fills in as results land. AI or Die is the anchor case study with the full narrative and the real numbers reported.

mainEntity.name: Who runs PodcastNetwork.org?

acceptedAnswer.text: Brett K Moore and Mike Partners are the co-founders, 50/50 equity split. Brett is co-founder and CEO. Brett operates from Hawaii and holds a portfolio of businesses including AI Expert and Apex Podcast Co. Mike is co-founder and holds a portfolio position with AI Expert as well. Both are co-authors of AI or Die, the anchor case study book. Both are directly involved in every Pre-Sold Author Package engagement.
